topic: Magnetic-gravity coupling constant kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal (post-EXP-201 dielectric-boundary-transition engagement)
change_summary: EXP-201 added a new paragraph engaging the dome's current 'dielectric boundary transition model' framing (live-site WIN-011 as of 2026-04-21). The paragraph credits the dome's epistemic update on WIN-011, then identifies three new problems — (i) magnitude mismatch between PRED-TIER-2 (-10 to -13 uGal) and the Mohe -6.5 uGal anchor of kappa; (ii) the pipeline rescue (Chebychev low-pass attenuation) suppresses exactly the contact-phase signal the dielectric model claims to predict; (iii) retaining kappa = 1.67 after abandoning the shielding interpretation it was derived under is parameter adoption, not derivation.
our_argument_summary: WIN-012's kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal is self-contradicted: (a) WIN-013/014 report 0.0 uGal eclipse gravity, making kappa's denominator vanish; (b) the dome's live-site 'dielectric boundary transition' reframing retains the numerical value of kappa while abandoning the mechanism from which it was derived (Unnikrishnan 2001-refuted gravitational shielding); (c) the dome's own forward prediction (PRED-TIER-2, -10 to -13 uGal) is 50-100% larger than the Mohe -6.5 uGal datum that anchors kappa; (d) the pipeline rescue that explains the 1999/2009 SG nulls ('attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release') is designed to suppress exactly the C1/C4 signal the new dielectric mechanism predicts.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (poller snapshot 2026-03-12 — stale for dielectric reframing)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No new monitor.py review this cycle — code_analysis tags unchanged from prior cycles. The tautological check (hardcoded 1.67 compared to hardcoded 10.9/6.5 = 1.677 within 5%) persists and is already described in detail_evidence. The new dielectric content is site-narrative, not code-driven; monitor.py has no dielectric-boundary-transition logic and no new prediction endpoint.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Still hardcoded self-referential identity check; kappa, BOU, and Mohe values all hardcoded in monitor.py.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The live site has added several escape clauses since c4: (1) WIN-011 dielectric-boundary-transition reframing; (2) WIN-013 'measurements were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation'; (3) WIN-014 '2009 campaign searched for totality-peak or shielding-style effects, not the contact-phase kappa-scaled pulse the ECM now predicts.' Our text now engages all three.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None on code substance. Note that raw-text/02-wins.txt was extracted 2026-03-12 and does not contain the dielectric-boundary-transition language; that language is only on the live site (WebFetch-verified 2026-04-21 by analyst EXP-201 and by curmudgeon c5). ISS-1285 already tracks the raw-text staleness. No WIN-012 finding depends on raw-text; safe.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from prior cycles — monitor.py hardcodes KAPPA=1.67, BOU=-10.9 nT, MOHE=-6.5 uGal, compares the ratio 10.9/6.5=1.677 against KAPPA within 5%. No live fetch. EXP-201 did not modify monitor.py or add any live-data endpoint.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics attributes the Yang & Wang 2002 Mohe C1/C4 valleys to thermal-tilt instrumental artifact of the LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter, and attributes the eclipse magnetic anomaly to Chapman-Sq-dynamo ionospheric currents. The dome relabels the same phenomenology as 'magnetic-gravity coupling' with coefficient kappa. The dielectric-boundary-transition reframing relabels again (now 'firmament-plate shading') but keeps the same numerical kappa — reinforcing rather than weakening the relabel classification.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: kappa = 1.67 is derived post-hoc from two published values (BOU 2017 -10.9 nT from Hayakawa et al. 2017-class papers; Mohe 1997 -6.5 uGal from Wang et al. 2000). Both observations predate the kappa definition. No pre-registration.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: kappa cannot be derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, or any dome-geometric parameter; it is a ratio of two independently measured values. EXP-201 strengthens this finding: the dome has now abandoned the mechanism (gravitational shielding) under which kappa was originally defined, but retains the numerical value — demonstrating that kappa is not a derivation from dome geometry but a residue of a discarded physics.

holes_found.description: 'at comparable latitude and instrument class' is unsupported by PRED-TIER-2's own specification. predictions.json PRED-TIER-2 reads only '10-13 uGal gravity pulse at C1/C4 eclipse contacts' — no station, no latitude, no instrument class specified. The Mohe 1997 datum was measured on a LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter at ~53.5 degrees N. The 1999/2009 SG nulls were superconducting gravimeters at Membach (~50 degrees N) and the Chinese network (variable latitudes). These are DIFFERENT instrument classes with different response times, noise characteristics, and contact-phase behavior. Our paragraph asserts 'comparable latitude and instrument class' without grounding — a dome defender can respond: 'PRED-TIER-2 is not specified to any particular instrument; our forward prediction may target spring gravimeters at ~53 degrees N where the Mohe signal was seen, making the -6.5 vs -10-13 uGal comparison invalid cross-instrument. Pick an instrument class or drop the claim.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) replace 'at comparable latitude and instrument class' with the specific instrument-class contrast we actually make later ('both are ground-based gravimeters responsive to contact-phase transients'), or (b) add a parenthetical: 'at the Mohe latitude class (~50-53 degrees N priority stations the dome's Chapman-Sq-fit methodology targets) on the two instrument classes whose data the dome invokes (spring gravimeters and SGs)'. Better: name the specific dome document that specifies PRED-TIER-2's target instruments, if one exists; if not, acknowledge the underspecification as its own problem — an eclipse gravity prediction that doesn't name its expected instrument class is a moving target.

holes_found.description: The kappa-arithmetic self-contradiction (delta-B/kappa = -10.2 to -12.6 uGal 'consistent with PRED-TIER-2 but incompatible with the Mohe datum') implicitly asserts the dome uses kappa in the PRED-TIER-2 derivation chain. But PRED-TIER-2's on-site derivation — per the dielectric-boundary-transition reframing — is a NEW mechanism (firmament-plate shading at C1/C4). A dome defender can say: 'PRED-TIER-2 is derived independently from the dielectric boundary transition physics. It happens to coincide with PRED-TIER-1 / kappa, but that is corroboration, not circularity. You are treating kappa as the derivation path for PRED-TIER-2; we derive PRED-TIER-2 from the dielectric model directly.' Our text does not foreclose this escape. If true, the self-contradiction collapses into 'two independent predictions that happen to agree via the same coupling ratio' — which actually STRENGTHENS the dome's case, not weakens it.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence anticipating this move: 'A dome defender will respond that PRED-TIER-2 is derived independently from the dielectric boundary transition mechanism, not from kappa — so the arithmetic is corroborative, not circular. But the dome site has published no independent dielectric-model derivation that yields -10 to -13 uGal from dome-geometric parameters (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric density at C1/C4). Absent such a derivation, the only quantitative bridge between the predicted magnetic delta-B and the predicted gravity delta-g IS kappa — which is defined by the Mohe -6.5 uGal datum. If the dome produces a genuine dielectric-model derivation of PRED-TIER-2 that does NOT pass through kappa, the internal contradiction dissolves; until it does, kappa is the bridge and the bridge is inconsistent with its own anchor.' This both anticipates the escape and tells the dome exactly what it would take to defeat our argument.

holes_found.description: 'Self-filtering detector' argument doesn't preempt the frequency-specific filter defender escape. The paragraph cites the dome's 'Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release' rescue and argues: the filter suppresses exactly the signal the dielectric model predicts. But a Chebychev low-pass filter is a FREQUENCY-domain filter, not a magnitude-domain or time-domain gate. A defender can respond: 'The Chebychev low-pass suppresses contact-phase transients ABOVE a certain frequency band (typical tidal-signal processing). A C1/C4 pulse of -10 to -13 uGal over several minutes has frequency content LOWER than whatever the filter cuts — so PRED-TIER-2 is falsifiable by SG data, and the 1999/2009 nulls mean the model is falsified, which we dispute on methodology grounds — not that PRED-TIER-2 is unfalsifiable by construction.' The bifurcation 'either falsifies the model or is unfalsifiable' misses this middle ground. Either the filter DOES pass pulses in the PRED-TIER-2 frequency band (in which case the 1999/2009 nulls do falsify the model), OR the filter is constructed to suppress exactly the frequency band where PRED-TIER-2 lives (in which case unfalsifiability holds). The dome has published no specific filter spec, so the defender can claim the former without being pinned.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the dichotomy by demanding specificity: 'A Chebychev low-pass has a stated cutoff frequency and a stated passband ripple; the dome has published neither. Without those specs, the rescue is whatever the dome needs it to be: if 1999/2009 SG nulls are cited as confirmations, the filter must have passed the ~few-minute contact-phase band (in which case PRED-TIER-2 should also pass and the nulls refute it); if 1999/2009 nulls are explained as contact-phase attenuation, the filter must suppress the ~few-minute band (in which case PRED-TIER-2 cannot be tested by any Level-3 SG record). The dome owes a specific cutoff frequency that makes both datasets simultaneously consistent with the model — or an acknowledgment that one of them is not a valid test.' This pins the defender on publishing the filter spec.

holes_found.description: Summary fields (claim, finding, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict) are still framed by the 6.5-vs-0.0 self-contradiction only; none surface the strongest post-EXP-201 arguments (dielectric reframing, parameter adoption, PRED-TIER-2 magnitude mismatch). c5 H9 flagged this before the EXP-201 integration and rated it minor — recommendation: optional. After EXP-201, the gap is larger because the detail now advances further than the summary. Still minor because (a) TLDR is meant to simplify, (b) the 6.5-vs-0.0 framing remains accurate, and (c) non-expert readers do not need the full dielectric-parameter-adoption argument. But a skeptical reader who sees only the summary, then reads the live-site WIN-011 dielectric reframing, will think our summary is outdated.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-sentence addendum to tldr_evidence: 'The dome's 2026-04 reframing to a 'dielectric boundary transition' mechanism retains the numerical kappa while abandoning the physics it was derived from.' Optional finding update: append ';the dome's 2026-04 dielectric-boundary-transition reframing retains kappa's number while abandoning its mechanism, and the dome's own PRED-TIER-2 forward prediction (-10 to -13 uGal) disagrees with the -6.5 uGal datum that anchors kappa.' Low priority — don't block on this.

holes_found.description: 'Credit where due' → 'curve-fit residue of a discarded physics' creates a rhetorical tension a charitable defender can exploit. We credit the dome for updating on Unnikrishnan 2001, then call the retained kappa value a 'curve-fit residue.' The defender position: 'You are attacking both the old and new framings. When we held the shielding interpretation, you refuted it via Unnikrishnan 2001. When we updated to the dielectric model, you call our retained parameter a curve-fit residue. No epistemic update satisfies you.' The exit for the dome, which our text gestures at but does not state explicitly, is: either re-derive kappa from dielectric physics OR drop kappa from the confirmed-WIN list until a re-derivation exists. EXP-201's anticipated-objection response made this explicit; the integrated paragraph does not.
holes_found.recommendation: Append a one-sentence constructive exit: 'The dome can complete the update in two ways that would satisfy this review: (a) publish a dome-geometric derivation of kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal from the dielectric-boundary-transition mechanism (independent of the two measurements used to compute it); or (b) demote WIN-012 to 'Pending' or 'Mechanism Contested' while the re-derivation is outstanding. Either move resolves the parameter-adoption problem; doing neither leaves kappa as a number without a current mechanism.' This softens the rhetorical tension by giving the dome a concrete route forward.

holes_found.description: The E-PRED-C phantom label (c5 H1 critical) is NOT present in the current detail_evidence. Direct search: zero matches for 'E-PRED-C' or 'predicts 0.0' anywhere in WIN-012's eight text fields. Commit b4fa494 (2026-04-21T06:39Z) already removed the phantom and replaced it with verified PRED-TIER-2 language. However, ISS-1325 (opened 2026-04-21T14:30Z) still asserts 'Fabricated E-PRED-C prediction label still in current detail_evidence' and EXP-206 was allocated to fix this non-existent problem. This is a DOUBLE-TRACK BUG: the decider opened a stale issue and allocated an EXP slot for work that is already done, AFTER the fix commit was already in git history. Trace: (a) 05:45Z curmudgeon c5 flagged E-PRED-C; (b) 06:39Z b4fa494 fixed it; (c) 14:30Z decider digested c5 as new open issues, including ISS-1325, despite the c5 finding already being patched. This is a recurrence of the 'digest does not cross-check against current state' bug class we should surface.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider action: close ISS-1325 as 'superseded by b4fa494' (already fixed); dissolve EXP-206 (no work remaining); optionally open a tinker issue to add a 'current-state cross-check' step to the c5 digest pipeline so that already-fixed findings do not reopen as issues. For the author of this audit: this finding should appear in the review output and in alerts.txt so the decider sees it on the next run.

stronger_arguments.description: Anchor-denominator identity: spell out that kappa is DEFINED as the ratio of two specific measurements, not as a coupling parameter that acquires meaning independent of its anchor. Compare to the fine-structure constant (dimensionless, measurable independently in many ways) — kappa has no independent measurement path. Changing the mechanism while retaining the ratio converts kappa from a phenomenological coupling into a numerological relic.
sources: Kostelecky, A. and S. Russell (2011), Rev. Mod. Phys. 83, 11 (SME phenomenological coupling framework — what a real phenomenological coupling looks like vs. what kappa does)
None needed — this is a methodological point about how coupling constants acquire physical meaning

stronger_arguments.description: Specification asymmetry between PRED-TIER-1 and PRED-TIER-2: PRED-TIER-1 specifies 'umbra-locked delta-B at >= 3 priority stations', which is genuinely prospective (our verdict in predictions.json: 'is_genuinely_prospective: true'). PRED-TIER-2 specifies only '10-13 uGal gravity pulse at C1/C4' — no stations, no instrument class, no latitude — and we verdict it 'recycled' because it restates WIN-011. A stronger argument: the asymmetry in specification IS the tell. If the dome had a real dielectric-model derivation of the gravity pulse, it would specify the observer class the way it specifies delta-B stations for PRED-TIER-1. The underspecification of PRED-TIER-2 is consistent with our claim that the -10 to -13 uGal value is an anchor-adjusted retrodiction scaled from PRED-TIER-1 via kappa, not an independent derivation.
sources: data/predictions.json PRED-TIER-1 and PRED-TIER-2 entries (internal verification)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author's update on WIN-011 is substantively honest epistemic behavior: acknowledged Unnikrishnan et al. (2001), explicitly relabeled WIN-011 'CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)', and proposed a dielectric-boundary-transition mechanism that PREDICTS a C1/C4 signal — matching the Yang & Wang (2002) observed timing pattern rather than the maximum-coverage pattern gravitational shielding would have required. The dome also committed to a specific falsifiable forward prediction for 2026-08-12 (PRED-TIER-2: -10 to -13 uGal at C1/C4). Both moves are epistemic-hygiene improvements. Our paragraph credits this explicitly ('Credit where due: ... moved in the empirically correct direction').
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The update is structurally incomplete. WIN-012's kappa was derived under the gravitational-shielding mechanism that has now been abandoned. Carrying the numerical value into the dielectric regime without re-derivation converts kappa from a mechanism-bearing coupling constant into a parameter residue. And the forward prediction the update commits to (PRED-TIER-2 -10 to -13 uGal) is 50-100% larger than the anchor datum (-6.5 uGal) from which kappa was computed. The same pipeline the dome invokes to explain 1999/2009 SG nulls would filter out the PRED-TIER-2 signal — making the rescue cannibalize the prediction. Honest epistemic updating of WIN-011 therefore strengthens rather than rescues the WIN-012 self-contradiction.

recommended_action_rationale: The EXP-201 paragraph materially strengthens the detail_evidence by engaging the dome's current position. The verdict (Self-Contradicted) still holds and does not require a change. The moderate holes above are tightenings, not rewrites: (a) softening or grounding 'comparable latitude and instrument class'; (b) anticipating the independent-dielectric-derivation escape; (c) tightening the self-filtering-detector dichotomy with a filter-spec demand. Plus two optional minor edits (summary/TLDR currency, rhetorical tension). None individually justify a major rewrite; together they justify a targeted EXP for minor edits. The E-PRED-C positive finding is a DECIDER action (close stale issue, dissolve unneeded EXP), not a content edit.
deeper_analysis: EXP-201 was the right move at the right time. The dome's live-site dielectric-boundary-transition reframing (2026-04-21) is an honest epistemic update that, left unengaged, would have left our review attacking a position the dome has already moved past — exactly the risk c5 H2 flagged. The EXP-201 paragraph lands on the dome's current position and extracts three distinct problems from the reframing: a quantitative mismatch (PRED-TIER-2 vs Mohe anchor), a structural trap (the pipeline rescue cannibalizes the forward prediction), and a methodological gap (parameter retention across mechanism change). The structure is clean and the tone is appropriately adversarial-but-charitable ('Credit where due'). Three moderate holes remain because the paragraph makes three specific claims that a defender can escape without our text having foreclosed the escape: (1) 'comparable latitude and instrument class' is an assertion PRED-TIER-2 doesn't back; (2) the kappa-arithmetic argument requires asserting the dome uses kappa in the PRED-TIER-2 derivation chain, which the dielectric framing explicitly denies; (3) the self-filtering detector dichotomy is bypassed by a frequency-band-specific filter interpretation that a defender can assert without our pinning it. All three have concrete tightenings that do not require rewriting the paragraph structure. Separately and more importantly, the decider has opened a stale critical issue (ISS-1325, E-PRED-C phantom) and allocated an EXP slot (EXP-206) for a problem that was fixed in b4fa494 before the digest even ran. This is a digest-pipeline bug the tinker should see — the curmudgeon c5 review was digested into new issues without cross-checking against the current wins.json state. Recommending the decider close ISS-1325 and dissolve EXP-206 as superseded, and consider a tinker audit of the digest's state-cross-check step.

findings: WIN-011 alignment: Our text correctly names the dome's CURRENT live WIN-011 position (dielectric boundary transition) and credits the update. No mischaracterization detected.

WIN-013/014 alignment: The retcon escape clauses we quote ('measurements were not optimized for C1/C4...' and '2009 campaign searched for totality-peak...') are verified in the c5 review's WebFetch session. Our text engages them appropriately.

WIN-025 (9-station Exhibit A storm data): Also depends on kappa = 1.67. If the parameter-adoption critique lands here, WIN-025 inherits it. This was noted in c5 as out-of-scope for EXP-201. Recommend the decider consider filing an issue against WIN-025 for the same kappa-mechanism-orphan critique.

advocate_mode.dome_defender_position: EXP-201 attacks a straw reading of our epistemic update. We conceded a mechanism change on WIN-011 — scientific behavior working as advertised. You then convert our concession into a parameter-adoption accusation, which is unfair: phenomenological coupling constants routinely persist across mechanism revisions (fine structure constant, G, etc.). PRED-TIER-2 is not derived from kappa; it is derived from the dielectric-boundary-transition mechanism and happens to coincide with delta-B/kappa because physics is self-consistent, not because we cheated. The -10 to -13 uGal prediction is for 2026-08-12 at specific priority stations (see PRED-TIER-1's named stations; PRED-TIER-2 inherits that station set). The SG pipeline filter is a standard tidal-processing Chebychev; it suppresses frequencies far below the contact-phase band. Your 'self-filtering detector' argument is a false dichotomy.
advocate_mode.our_response: Three specific responses. (1) Fine-structure constant has multiple independent measurement paths (spectroscopy, QHE, g-2); kappa has one — its defining ratio. Dropping kappa from a mechanism it was derived under is not like carrying alpha across QED/QCD; it is like keeping a curve-fit slope after discarding the dataset. (2) If PRED-TIER-2 is independently derived from the dielectric mechanism, publish the derivation. It is not on the live site as of 2026-04-21. Until it is, the only quantitative bridge between delta-B and delta-g is kappa, and that bridge fails. (3) The dome has published no Chebychev cutoff frequency. We will close the dichotomy the moment the filter spec appears; until then, the 'frequency-band-specific escape' is handwaving. If PRED-TIER-2 inherits PRED-TIER-1's station set, say so in PRED-TIER-2's text — current text specifies neither stations nor instruments.
advocate_mode.remaining_tension: The dome defender's independent-dielectric-derivation claim is empirically testable — either the derivation appears on the live site or it doesn't. Our review is currently on the right side of that test, but should explicitly invite the dome to produce the derivation.

quantitative_verification.kappa_arithmetic: delta-B/kappa with PRED-TIER-1 (-17 to -21 nT) and kappa = 1.67 nT/uGal: -17/1.67 = -10.18 uGal, -21/1.67 = -12.57 uGal. Paragraph states '-10.2 to -12.6 uGal'. Check: correct.
quantitative_verification.magnitude_ratio: -10 vs -6.5: 10/6.5 = 1.54 (54% larger magnitude). -13 vs -6.5: 13/6.5 = 2.0 (100% larger magnitude). Paragraph states '50-100% larger'. Check: 54-100% is accurately described as '50-100%'.
quantitative_verification.pred_tier_2_verdict: our_verdict in data/predictions.json: 'recycled'. dome_confidence: 'MEDIUM'. restates_win: 'WIN-011'. All consistent with our characterization.
